text:
Market today: Cautiously optimistic tone. JP Morgan raised its S&P 500 target, which lifts sentiment for your QQQ/SPY anchors, but they flagged flash-crash risk — real concern on margin. The OpenAI/Broadcom chip news and SK Hynix's massive $29B Nasdaq listing are the real drivers for your chip/tech names today.

Your holdings: AVGO (your largest, -11.56%) needs eyes — it's already flagged TRIM, and the OpenAI chip deal with Broadcom could cut both ways (validation vs. margin pressure). PLTR (-11.15%) is flagged SELL with no obvious catalyst to reverse it today. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec already near its $9.50 stop — on margin, one bad tick and you're stopped out hard. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile IPO with thin data; treat it like it can gap 10% either direction overnight.

Watch: 1) AVGO reaction to the Broadcom/OpenAI chip news — this could move your largest position meaningfully. 2) SK Hynix Nasdaq listing impact on SMH and chip sentiment broadly.

Bottom line: Margin amplifies every mistake — act on PLTR and AVGO before the market decides for you.
